review_title: Convenient but could be more transparent
description: Ive used Plaid a bunch of times to link my bank account to different apps like budgeting tools, investing platforms. Most of the time, it works smoothly. Way better than having to enter everything manually. That said, it’s not perfect. Some smaller banks or credit unions aren’t supported, and sometimes you have to reconnect accounts if the link breaks. Also, it’s not always super clear what data the app is getting access to. Security-wise, I feel pretty good about it. They don’t store your login info, and everything seems encrypted and secure. Still, I wish there were more user-facing tools to manage connections across different apps in one place. Overall, Plaid makes connecting your bank to apps fast and easy. It mostly just works, which is kind of the point. A few more transparency and control features would make it better
author: Dallas B.
pros: -Most connections are stable and don’t need frequent re-linking. -Works with most major services like Venmo, Robinhood, Mint, and many more -Login details are not stored on Plaid’s servers
cons: -Not every institution is available, especially local or regional ones. -There’s no single place for users to manage or revoke app permissions across all Plaid-connected apps

review_title: Great for financial integrations but occasional connection issues
description: My experiance with Plaid has been mostly positive. It’s a very useful platform for connecting financial data and powering apps like payments, budgeting, and investing tools. The API capabilities are pretty strong and allow companies to build advanced financial features without dealing with banks directly. That said, it’s not always perfect and sometimes requires extra handling for edge cases or connection issues. But overall it’s a reliable and widely used solution in the fintech space.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I liked how easy it makes connecting bank accounts to apps. The whole process is pretty quick and smooth and it works with a huge number of banks which is really helpful. It saves a lot of time compared to manually entering details or verifying accounts. I also like that it feels secure and widely trusted since a lot of popular app use it. Overall it just makes financial integrations much simpler for both users and developers.
cons: One thing I didn’t like is that sometimes bank connections can break or not sync properly, especially with smaller banks. It can be a bit frustrating when data is delayed or missing and you don’t realize it right away. Also, for businesses, pricing and setup can feel a little complex depending on the use case. There are also some concerns people have around sharing financial data, even though it’s generally secure.

review_title: It’s convenient, but it could use some work
description: Although my overall experience with plaid is about 75% it’s a wonderful platform to verify your banking information fairly quickly. I just worry about other people that may have an old number of mine having access to my old banking information.
author: Jamie H.
pros: The take away from plaid is having all of your immediate banking information and verification at your fingertips. And it’s fairly easy to use.
cons: A few cons that I have about plat is the fact that you cannot verify Cash app or PayPal 70% of people use those platforms however they’re not compatible with plaid. Another thing that I disliked is that you’re begging information is linked to that number until you physically remove it. I’ve had instances where I had a new number and I had to verify my bank through Platt and someone else else’s banking information popped up.

description: It has been a positive experience! Our information has been kept secure while connecting us to the intended platform.
author: Savannah R.
pros: This was an easy way to securely link our business bank account to the software that will take payments from customers. The process was very efficient!
cons: We have not experienced any cons while using this to connect our bank account securely to the system that accepts payments from our customers.

description: Overall Plaid has provided a smooth and reliable way to link financial accounts and power modern financial apps.
author: Haley D.
pros: Plaid makes it incredibly easy to securely connect bank accounts to apps with fast integration and support.
cons: Some employees in my company have worried about sharing sensitive financial data however that would be an issue with many other bank connecting applications as well.

author: Sean C.
pros: Plaid is a fantastic banking and payment tool, helping to seamlessly integrate bank accounts safely. Customer support is also fantastic
cons: No real downsides to note. Any small issue I had was easily remedied by customer support and no issues further happened

review_title: Easy transition from one account to another.
description: I’m overall my experience with plaid seems fairly easy. It’s very quick to set up and there are minimal issues or glitches.
author: Amy S.
pros: I really like that Plaid allows seamless transition between bank accounts and financial service products.
cons: It does Sometimes require a few logins, but that may be more reflective of the platforms themselves.

review_title: Plaid keeps your identity safe, easily!
description: My overall experience with Plaid has been great! I know my information is safe when I need to log in to third party banking and the ease of use is fantastic!
author: Jamie L.
pros: What I like most about Plaid is the ease of use, especially when logging into secure online banking from third parties. I can be sure my information is safe when I use Plaid.
cons: I have no complaints about Plaid. Plaid is a great program to use when needing secure online banking authorization.

description: Plaid is an Easy, safe, secured reliable and accessible form of bill payment. I would encourage others who maybe looking tor the features to give it a try.
author: Catheryn S.
pros: confident using Plaid because it encrypts connections and doesn’t permanently store bank login info. once connected, the payment process — including ACH-based bill payments — generally works without major hassles and avoids repeated manual input. ￼
cons: Smaller banks and credit unions are not supported through here and that can be difficult to navigate

description: Had a great experience me and my wife both used it to transfer and deposit money would highly recommend to anybody with a small business
author: Alex A.
pros: Really love this for payment processing great for transferring money. Online banking made easy direct deposit deposits. Super helpful customer support value for the money cannot be beat and super easy to use.
cons: Really didn’t find anything I disliked about it. It took me a minute to figure everything out, but had a really good experience overall
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The pay as you go feature is the best, Connecting your banking can be simple. Secure system that is easy to navigate.
author: Shannel H.
pros: Pay as you go is the best feature. Easy to move money and refunding is made easy. Not much on the banking side, but pretty easy to use.
cons: Customer support is ok, helpful but can be better. Errors with synching and having to re-do to get the error to go away.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Kelly W.
pros: I love that it is able to connect my bank with any app that uses Plaid.
cons: I do not have any cons or complaints about Plaid.

description: It's worked well connecting business account information with different platforms. Sometimes it requires a few logins, but that may be more reflective of the platforms themselves.
author: Damon B.
pros: It seamlessly connects different payment platforms.
cons: The log-in setup sometimes requires a few tries.

review_title: Convenient, but not upfront with consumers
author: Leahya W.
pros: Plaid allows seamless transition between bank accounts and financial service products. Plaid incorporates both verification and account linking in the fastest manner possible.
cons: The only issue I have with Plaid is that it does not make it clear that they store data on consumer spending. This causes disgruntled consumers, which cannot be fixed after the information has been taken.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Fast and Easy way to process payments
description: My go to for payment processing. Easy to use, but some banks don't work with it. All major banks do work with it but state based ones are a gamble.
author: Cassidy S.
pros: So easy to use! I love how it integrates within seconds to transfer money from your bank of choice. Very cheap compared to other softwares.
cons: Refunds take awhile but customer service is great! Super friendly and quick turnaround to get back to you.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall, plaid is an easy simple way to pay. I love when plaid is an option.
author: Ethan L.
pros: Ease of use, connects directly to my bank account + makes paying easy.
cons: The only thing is that I prefer to give my sensitive info away to as few people as possible. But it’s a trusted company so I dont mind.

description: It was fine. Safe, secure and didn't have any issues.
author: Shawnalee K.
pros: I was a user of plaid when I was a bank of america customer and had to use PLAID to verify my account several times. I also had to use Plaid in order to pay for my orders through Walmart (if I attached my bank account instead of my card). I was able to access my transaction history up to 24 months, never had any fraud issues. My income validation always was accurate as well.
cons: It doesn't utilize all bank accounts-only some. I was lucky to be able to use my bank of america account.

description: I like plaid because it’s very easy and convenient to use. Plaid helps you login with having to enter all your bank information like by your phone number or email. I personally think it’s very easy to use and really convenient
author: Ashley C.
pros: Plaid automatically connects your bank account with your email or phone number. It’s a faster and safer and it’s the most trusted financial platform that most people use.
cons: There’s nothing that I don’t like about plaid

description: My overall experience with plaid is not the greatest tbh. They have prevented me from being able to connect my accounts on multiple occasions because their network of partnering banks is minimal.
author: Keri M.
pros: What I like most about plaid is that it allows external banks to connect with other apps for funding purposes or transferring funds securely.
cons: What I don't like about plaid,is that it's very specific on the banks it allows us to connect to 3rd party apps. Unfortunately most of the banks I have access to are not supported so it's harder for me to truly appreciate what I'm sure could be a good potential app.

description: I’ve had a decent experience with them and nothing of mine was ruined
author: Akiya S.
pros: I like that it’s a secure website for you banking and to login to your account
cons: There are always a lot of data leaks with the company

review_title: Plaid is the only game in town for connecting to any financial service
description: We use Plaid to check bank balances and to accept ACH payments. It works great for both purposes. Be aware that some financial platforms (banks) are hostile to Plaid (to the extent of suing Plaid) and have sporadic availability. Some platforms have an arrangement with Plaid that requires you to complete additional documentation steps, like sharing your security policies and business information. Presumably, you get more reliable integration in exchange. For our use case, the cost of Plaid is trivial, but to accept ACH payments, Plaid integrates with Stripe, which does charge a fee for the actual payment. Bottom line, no other service can offer what Plaid can, so if you want your customers to connect to their financial institution to your product, Plaid is your only option.
author: David V.
pros: Plaid is relatively easy to setup and use. The API documentation is great and API credential setup is easy. The SDK supports all common platforms, and rich code samples are provided.
cons: Getting approved for production access was a paid. The support team ignored requests. We had to create a second account, which was finally approved. Why this was needed is a mystery. Plaid will push an expensive monthly plan, but it's not needed for sporadic access.

description: As a solo traveler for the past month, Plaid has been required on all Airbnb experiences I've booked. Despite being logged into the same account, on the same computer, without having ever cleared my cookies or history during this time, I've been required to reauthenticate my info by linking up my account to Wells Fargo each and every time I book. It would be annoying enough already, but then it doesn't even work half the time: crashes the tab, stalls, and you're left staring at the screen wondering if you should close the tab and start over or...? Gah! Airbnb used to have a regular check-out process with normal credit card processing. I wonder why on earth this Plaid nightmare is needed? Someone explain it to me. I have resorted to searching Airbnb for unique experiences, and then when I'm ready to book, I copy & paste the title into Google just in case I can book it on literally ANY other platform that doesn't use Plaid. What did we do to deserve this torture, Airbnb? Haven't we been good to you? Why such punishment for our loyalty to you! :(
author: Virginia J.
pros: Nothing nothing nothing nothing nothing nothing
cons: Lagging, crashing, stalling, slow, redundant, repetitive, exhausting
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Plaid is great. I wish more options/places were listed & some already listed to be more detailed so that you don't accidentally go to the wrong login. More options/places are added constantly so its hard to complain about an app that is constantly trying to improve
author: Melissa T.
pros: I like how it recognizes the different needs & requirements each individual financial group/bank expects. Some simply need login information while others demand more details. Either way, Plaid requires that extra level of security to be sure & safe which helps release any worry about identity theft in my opinion
cons: While Plaid lists tons of different banks & financial institutions its also missing a good number of them too. Not just by their doing but by the intended group & their exoectations

review_title: A fantastic partner for development integrations that is quick and secure
author: Parth K.
pros: When conducting business with your business, Plaid is a safe and secure solution for your consumers to verify their financial details. It features a user-friendly interface for both myself and my clients. Each issue we initiate with Plaid's support staff receives prompt attention, and they take the time to clarify faults and provide solutions. They consistently follow up to make sure that the problems of our users have been fixed and see cases through to completion.
cons: Sometimes it takes months to remedy a problem. By the time a user's issue is rectified, they may have given up and terminated their accounts. Plaid provides a wide range of features, many of which small company owners won't require. This gives the setup procedure a more difficult than necessary appearance. Keep your APIs straightforward, and you won't experience any headaches.

description: Great i love this product.
author: Kerby R.
pros: Very fast and very accurate. Saves a lot of time verifying bank accounts.
cons: I really wish they had an app. So when you're out of the office you can still check.
